source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
title: Ensure Radius server is using the recommended security protocol

input_row.source: CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
input_row.title: Ensure Radius server is using the recommended security protocol
input_row.description: The authentication protocol between the Microsoft AD DCs and the RADIUS server supported are PAP, CHAP, MS-CHAPv1, and MS-CHAPv2.
input_row.rationale: MS-CHAPv2 provides the strongest security of the options supported.
input_row.audit: Perform the steps to check multi-factor authentication using the radius server protocol is set to MS-CHAP v2. From the Console: For AWS Managed AD based environments; 1. Log in to the Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. Choose the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directory details page, select the Networking & security tab. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section, confirm that the Protocol is set to MS- CHAPv2. For directory connector / self-managed AD environments 1. Log in to the AWS Workspaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. Select the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directories page, scroll to the Multi-factor authentication section and select Edit. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section confirm that, in the Protocol field MS- CHAPv2 is selected from the dropdown list. If it is not set to MS-CHAPv2 refer to the remediation steps below. Internal Only - General
input_row.remediation: Perform the steps below to set the protocol to MS-CHAPv2 for multi-factor authentication. From the console: For AWS Managed AD based environments; 1. Log in to the Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. On the Directory details page, select the Networking & security tab. 4. In the Multi-factor authentication section, choose Actions, and then choose Edit. 5. On the Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) page change the following value: 6. Protocol - MS-CHAPv2 7. Click Save. For directory connector / self-managed AD environments 1. Log in to the AWS Workspaces console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/workspaces 2. In the left pane select Directories. 3. Select the directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory. 4. On the Directories page, scroll to the Multi-factor authentication section and select Edit. 5. In the Multi-factor authentication section modify the protocol using the dropdown menu to be MS-CHAPv2 from the currently selected option. 6. Click Save once settings are as desired.
input_row.justification: Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS does not currently expose the RADIUS server security protocol configuration (specifically MS-CHAPv2 for MFA) via any public API, AWS CLI, or SDK. This setting is only accessible and configurable through the AWS Management Console UI for Directory Service and WorkSpaces. There is no AWS Config rule or resource property that can be queried or monitored for this protocol setting, making full automation impossible at this time.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Log in to the AWS Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2. 2. In the left pane, select "Directories." 3. Click on the relevant Directory. On the Directory details page, select the "Networking & security" tab. 4. In the "Multi-factor authentication" section, choose "Actions," then "Edit." 5. On the "Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA)" page, verify that the "Protocol" is set to "MS-CHAPv2." 6. If not set, change the protocol to "MS-CHAPv2" and save the configuration. 7. Confirm that the protocol change has been saved and is reflected in the configuration. 8. Document the setting (screenshot or note) for audit evidence.
input_row.default_value: By default, this is dependent on your RADIUS server.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the AWS Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2.
2. In the left pane, select "Directories."
3. Click on the relevant Directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory.
4. On the Directory details page, select the "Networking & security" tab.
5. In the "Multi-factor authentication" section, choose "Actions," then "Edit."
6. Verify that the "Protocol" is set to "MS-CHAPv2." 
7. If it is not set to "MS-CHAPv2," change the protocol using the dropdown menu.
8. Click "Save" once the settings are configured correctly.
9. Confirm that the protocol change has been saved and is reflected in the configuration.
10. Document the setting (screenshot or note) for audit evidence.

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The decision is based on the fact that the control regarding the RADIUS server's security protocol (specifically requiring it to be MS-CHAPv2) cannot be queried or managed via AWS APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI. The configuration can only be accessed and modified through the AWS Management Console, making a manual audit necessary.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** PARTIALLY_AGREE

- The classification of the audit approach as Manual is mostly correct given the current AWS capabilities. However, it is important to note that while the AWS Management Console is the primary method for managing this specific configuration, AWS Config can be used to monitor changes in AWS Directory Service settings. AWS Config rules can be created to ensure compliance with specific configurations, though direct API calls to modify the RADIUS protocol are not available. Therefore, while the initial setup and verification are manual, compliance monitoring can be automated.

- As of the latest AWS updates, there are no specific boto3 methods or AWS CLI commands that allow direct querying or modification of the RADIUS protocol setting for AWS Managed Microsoft AD. This limitation justifies the manual approach for initial configuration verification.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8

- The manual steps provided are accurate and complete for verifying and modifying the RADIUS protocol setting via the AWS Management Console.
- The program name convention is not applicable here as this is a manual process. However, if automation were possible, a descriptive name such as `aws_directoryservice_radius_protocol_check` would be appropriate.
- The assessment correctly identifies the lack of direct API support for this configuration.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- Consider integrating AWS Config to monitor changes to the Directory Service settings, which can provide alerts if the protocol is changed from the desired configuration. This would enhance the compliance posture by providing ongoing monitoring capabilities.
- Include a note about the potential use of AWS CloudTrail to log changes made in the AWS Management Console, which could serve as an additional audit trail.
- Suggest documenting the rationale for selecting MS-CHAPv2 over other protocols, emphasizing security best practices.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- I am confident in the assessment's conclusion regarding the manual approach due to the current limitations of AWS APIs in managing this specific configuration.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:** Modify

- Keep the manual approach for initial configuration verification as is, but enhance the audit process by integrating AWS Config for continuous compliance monitoring. Document the use of AWS CloudTrail for logging changes and consider providing a detailed explanation of why MS-CHAPv2 is the recommended protocol from a security perspective.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Manual

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the current AWS capabilities do not support direct API calls to query or modify the RADIUS protocol setting for AWS Managed Microsoft AD. The configuration must be accessed and verified through the AWS Management Console, as there are no available boto3 methods or AWS CLI commands for this specific task. While AWS Config can monitor changes, the initial setup and verification remain a manual process.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Log in to the AWS Directory Service console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/directoryservicev2.
2. In the left pane, select "Directories."
3. Click on the relevant Directory ID link for your AWS Managed Microsoft AD directory.
4. On the Directory details page, select the "Networking & security" tab.
5. In the "Multi-factor authentication" section, choose "Actions," then "Edit."
6. Verify that the "Protocol" is set to "MS-CHAPv2."
7. If it is not set to "MS-CHAPv2," change the protocol using the dropdown menu.
8. Click "Save" once the settings are configured correctly.
9. Confirm that the protocol change has been saved and is reflected in the configuration.
10. Document the setting (screenshot or note) for audit evidence.

**WHY NOT AUTOMATED:** The RADIUS protocol setting for AWS Managed Microsoft AD is not exposed through AWS SDK APIs, boto3, or AWS CLI, making it impossible to automate this check. The configuration can only be accessed and modified via the AWS Management Console.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** The second AI opinion suggested partial automation using AWS Config for monitoring, but this does not apply to the initial verification and setup, which must be done manually. Therefore, the final decision is to classify this control as a manual process.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS END USER COMPUTE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.2.0
